2606.27460.title: Developmental approach reveals the statistical learning of Neural Language Models: Transformers generalize from the most abstract statistical patterns
2606.27460.paper_summary: In this study, we use a developmental approach to investigate the statistical learning and mental representation of neural language models (NLM). A series of Generative Transformer models are trained on a synthetic grammar. The model states are saved at multiple stages in the course of training. Through analyzing how the internal representations of these models change in the developmental path, we found that NLMs acquire the most abstract global statistical knowledge at the beginning of learning and later acquire the relatively local statistical dependencies. This learning path contains many over-generalizations from the very beginning and these over-generalizations are gradually constrained in the later stage of learning. Based on this observation, we propose a new framework to explain the statistical learning and language cognition of NLMs.
2606.27460.paper_summary_zh: 在這項研究中，我們採用發展性的方法來探討神經語言模型（NLM）的統計學習和心理表徵。<lb/>一系列生成式Transformer模型在合成語法上進行訓練。<lb/>模型狀態在訓練過程中的多個階段被保存。<lb/>通過分析這些模型的內部表徵在發展路徑中的變化，我們發現NLM在學習初期獲得了最抽象的全球統計知識，隨後才獲得相對局部的統計依賴。<lb/>這一學習路徑從一開始就包含了許多過度概括，而這些過度概括在學習的後期逐漸受到限制。<lb/>基於這一觀察，我們提出了一個新框架來解釋NLM的統計學習和語言認知。
2606.27460.author: Wang Bojun et.al.
2606.27460.authors: Wang Bojun, Holly Jenkins, Elizabeth Wonnacott
